name: Scrambled Eggs
instructions: Crack eggs into a bowl with milk and a pinch of salt. Whisk until fully combined and smooth.
Melt butter in a non-stick pan over the lowest heat — patience makes perfect scrambled eggs.
Pour in eggs. Using a silicone spatula, gently push curds from edges toward the centre in slow sweeping motions.
Remove from heat while eggs still look slightly underdone — residual heat finishes them perfectly.
Season with black pepper, garnish with chives, and serve immediately on warm toast.

name: French Toast
allIngredients: 2 thick slices of bread (brioche is ideal)
instructions: Whisk eggs, milk, sugar, and cinnamon together in a wide shallow bowl until combined.
Soak each bread slice for 30 seconds per side, pressing gently so the custard absorbs fully.
Melt butter in a pan over medium heat until foaming subsides.
Cook the soaked bread 2–3 minutes per side until deeply golden.
Dust with powdered sugar and serve warm with maple syrup or fresh fruit.

name: Fluffy Pancakes
instructions: Whisk all dry ingredients together in a large bowl.
Beat eggs, milk, and melted butter in a separate bowl.
Pour wet into dry and fold gently — lumps are fine. Overmixing makes tough pancakes. Rest batter 5 minutes.
Heat a buttered pan over medium-low. Pour ¼ cup batter per pancake.
Cook until bubbles form on the surface and edges look set. Flip once and cook 1 minute more.

name: Avocado Toast
instructions: Toast bread until deeply golden and firm enough to hold the topping.
Halve the avocado, remove the pit, and scoop flesh into a bowl.
Add lemon juice and a generous pinch of salt. Mash to your preferred texture.
Spread onto toast. Finish with chilli flakes and a drizzle of olive oil.

name: Banana Oatmeal
instructions: Combine oats, milk, and a pinch of salt in a small saucepan over medium heat.
Stir frequently as mixture heats, about 4–5 minutes, until oats are thick and creamy.
Remove from heat. Stir in half the banana slices and the honey.
Top with remaining banana and a dusting of cinnamon. Serve warm.

name: Grilled Cheese
instructions: Butter the outer faces of both bread slices generously and evenly.
Arrange cheese between the unbuttered inner sides.
Place in a cold pan butter-side down. Turn heat to medium-low. Cook 3–4 minutes until golden.
Flip carefully and cook the other side 2–3 minutes until golden and cheese is fully melted.
Rest 1 minute before cutting so the cheese sets slightly.

name: Caesar Salad
instructions: Make dressing: whisk egg yolks, mustard, and lemon juice. Slowly drizzle in olive oil while whisking constantly to emulsify.
Stir in minced garlic. Season with black pepper and salt to taste.
For croutons: tear sourdough into chunks, toss in olive oil, bake at 200°C for 10–12 minutes until golden.
Toss romaine with enough dressing to coat — don't overdress.
Top with shaved Parmesan and croutons. Serve immediately.

name: Tomato Soup
instructions: Melt butter in a large pot over medium heat. Sweat onion for 8 minutes until completely soft and translucent.
Add garlic and cook 2 more minutes until fragrant.
Add tomatoes with their liquid. Season with salt. Simmer uncovered 15 minutes.
Blend until very smooth using an immersion or standing blender.
Stir in cream and warm through. Adjust seasoning and serve with fresh basil.

name: BLT Sandwich
instructions: Cook bacon in a dry pan over medium-high heat until deeply crisp. Drain on paper towels.
Toast bread to your preference — sturdy and golden.
Spread mayonnaise generously on both toasted slices.
Season tomato slices with salt and pepper. Layer lettuce, tomato, then bacon.
Press together firmly, cut diagonally, and serve immediately.

name: Egg Fried Rice
instructions: Heat a wok or wide pan over the highest heat until visibly smoking.
Add oil, pour in beaten eggs, scramble until just set, break into pieces and remove.
Add more oil. Fry garlic for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add cold rice, pressing against the hot surface to develop a slight crust. Toss every 30 seconds for 3 minutes.
Return eggs, add soy sauce, toss to combine. Finish with sesame oil and spring onions.

name: Spaghetti Carbonara
instructions: Boil spaghetti in heavily salted water until al dente. Reserve 1 cup pasta water before draining.
Render pancetta in a cold pan over medium heat until fat is translucent and edges are crisp. Remove from heat.
Whisk egg yolks, whole egg, and both cheeses with generous black pepper.
Add hot drained pasta to the pancetta pan off heat. Toss to coat in the rendered fat.
Add egg mixture and a splash of pasta water. Toss vigorously — residual heat creates a silky sauce without scrambling. Add pasta water gradually until glossy. Serve immediately.

name: Chicken Stir-Fry
allIngredients: Mixed vegetables (bell pepper, broccoli, snap peas)
instructions: Toss chicken with 1 tbsp soy sauce and a pinch of cornstarch. Rest 10 minutes.
Heat wok until smoking. Add oil. Sear chicken in a single layer without moving for 2 minutes until golden. Toss and cook 1 minute more. Remove.
Stir-fry garlic and ginger for 30 seconds. Add vegetables and toss on high heat 2 minutes.
Return chicken with remaining soy sauce. Toss to combine and serve over rice.

name: Mushroom Risotto
instructions: Sauté mushrooms in olive oil on high heat until golden and all moisture has evaporated, about 8 minutes. Season with salt and set aside.
Sweat shallot in butter in a heavy-bottomed pan. Add garlic, then rice. Toast rice 2 minutes stirring constantly.
Deglaze with white wine and stir until absorbed.
Add warm stock one ladle at a time, stirring and waiting for each addition to absorb — about 18–20 minutes total.
Off heat, beat in cold butter and Parmesan vigorously until glossy. Fold in mushrooms. Rest 2 minutes before serving.

name: Vegetable Fried Rice
instructions: Heat oil in wok on highest heat. Scramble eggs until just set, break into pieces, remove.
Fry garlic and onion until fragrant, about 2 minutes.
Add frozen vegetables and stir-fry 2 minutes until heated through.
Add cold rice, pressing against the pan to develop a slight crust. Toss every 30 seconds for 3 minutes.
Add soy sauce and return eggs. Toss to combine. Finish with sesame oil.

name: French Onion Soup
instructions: Melt butter and oil together in a heavy pot over the lowest heat. Cook onions 45–55 minutes, stirring every 10 minutes, until deeply caramelised and mahogany in colour.
Deglaze with white wine, scraping up all fond from the bottom. Reduce by half.
Add stock and thyme. Simmer 20 minutes. Season generously with salt and pepper.
Ladle soup into oven-safe crocks. Float toasted baguette slices on top. Cover completely with Gruyère.
Broil on high heat 3–5 minutes until cheese is bubbling and spotted with brown. Serve immediately — crocks will be very hot.

name: Baked Salmon
allIngredients: Salt and freshly cracked black pepper
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C. Line a baking tray with parchment paper.
Pat salmon dry with paper towels. Rub with olive oil, garlic, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Place fillets skin-side down on the tray. Lay lemon rounds on top.
Bake 12–15 minutes until salmon flakes easily with a fork but the centre is still slightly translucent.
Garnish with fresh dill and serve with roasted vegetables or steamed rice.
